title: How Our Eyes Came From a One-Eyed Creature
summary: Did you know that your eyes might have come from a creature with only one eye? Scientists found that a very old animal, like a tiny worm, had a single eye on top of its head. This animal lived about 600 million years ago. It used to have two eyes, but it lost them because it didn't need them. Later, it grew a single eye in the middle of its head. This eye could tell light from dark. After a long time, this creature became active again and needed better eyes. The single eye changed into two eyes, like the ones we have today. That's why our eyes are different from insect eyes. Our eyes grew from our brain, but insect eyes grew from their skin.

keywords.explanation: a family member who lived a long time ago
the part of the body you see with
the brightness that lets you see
the organ inside your head that thinks and controls your body
the back part of the eye that catches light

questions.question: What did the ancient creature have on top of its head?
How long ago did this creature live?
Why did the creature lose its two eyes?
What could the single eye do?
Where did our eyes grow from?
Where do insect eyes come from?

background_read: All animals have ancestors that lived long ago. Some ancestors looked very different from today's animals. This story is about how our eyes evolved from a one-eyed creature. Evolution is how living things change over time to survive better.
Article_Structure: WHO: A tiny worm-like creature that lived 600 million years ago.
WHAT: It had a single eye on top of its head, which later became our eyes.
WHERE: In the ocean, where it filtered plankton from seawater.
WHY: Because it lost its two eyes and then grew a single eye, which eventually turned into two eyes again.

perspectives.description: They were surprised by this discovery and say it changes how we understand eye evolution.
Animals change over time to adapt to their environment, and this creature's eyes changed as it became more active.
